title: Celtis hasseltii
intro: Celtis hasseltii, also known as the Hasselt's nettle tree or Hasselt's hackberry, is a deciduous tree species native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, headache, and inflammation, as well as promoting digestive health and reducing coughs. The bioactive constituents of Celtis hasseltii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Celtis hasseltii is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, often in combination with other herbs, to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Celtis hasseltii is classified as a species of plant in the family Cannabaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as hemp or nettle family. However, some sources may place it in the family Ulmaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as elm family. The genus Celtis is a group of plants within the family Ulmaceae. Celtis hasseltii is native to Southeast Asia. It is a deciduous tree species.
uses: Celtis hasseltii, also known as Hasselt's nettle tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves, bark, and roots are often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Celtis hasseltii is also used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effects on human health.
